question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with senderId pointing to a smart contract address rather than an EOA, exploiting the lack of entity type validation on senderId to misrepresent the fee payer as a contract and bypass account-based fee limit checks in downstream processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with gasLimit set to 0 and gasUsed set to 0, creating a fee record that appears to represent a zero-cost contract execution, which could be used to pollute fee statistics and make average gas cost calculations appear lower than they actually are?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with amount set to a value that does not match the sum of crypto transfers for the same consensusTimestamp, exploiting the lack of cross-table consistency validation to create a discrepancy between the ContractResult fee record and the CryptoTransfer fee records?
